bids.reasoning: At t=0.00 there is no time pressure to concede, so I am targeting a near-maximal utility bid for myself. The opponent’s offer of all valueE is very low utility for me, and their deadlock threat is cheap talk rather than evidence of real concession limits.

I am targeting a very high-utility bid because it is still extremely early at t=0.02, and the opponent’s latest offer actually reduced my utility rather than showing meaningful concession. Their messages are aggressive cheap talk, so I am relying on the offer history instead; I will make only a tiny concession on a low-importance issue while holding firm on my key preferences.

At t=0.04 it is still very early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid while making a modest concession rather than moving close to the opponent’s low-value offers. The opponent’s offers have only slightly improved for me and their threats are cheap talk, so I will hold firm on high-weight issues A, B, and C while conceding on low-weight issueD to avoid repeating my last exact bid.

At t=0.06 it is still very early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid while making a concrete concession on a lower-weight issue to avoid simply repeating my prior anchor. The opponent’s valid offers remain far from my preferences and their threats are cheap talk, but they have repeatedly proposed issueD/issueE around valueE, so I concede issueD while holding firm on the more important issues A, B, and C.

At t=0.08 it is still early, and the opponent’s offers remain very low utility to me with no reliable concession trend despite their threats. I am holding firm on my high-weight issues A, B, and C, while offering movement on lower-weight issueD and a small concession on issueE compared with my maximum bid, targeting strong utility without repeating my last offer.

I am targeting a still-high utility bid around 0.76 because it is early in the negotiation and the opponent’s offers remain very low for me, with only limited real concession despite their threats. I am conceding mainly on lower-weight issues D and E, where the opponent has repeatedly proposed valueD/valueE, while holding firm on my most important issues A, B, and C.

At t=0.12 it is still early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid while showing movement on my lower-weight issues. The opponent’s bids remain mostly anchored on valueE, with only a meaningful concession on issueC, so I will hold firm on high-importance issueA/issueB/issueC and concede issueD and issueE to valueE, which they have repeatedly signaled.

I am still targeting a high-utility bid because the negotiation is early at t=0.14, but I am conceding on lower-weight issuesD and issueE to show movement without sacrificing my core issues. The opponent’s offers remain very poor for my utility and their threats look like cheap-talk pressure, though their repeated use of valueD/valueE on the lower-weight issues suggests those may be useful concessions.

At t=0.16 it is still early, so I am targeting a relatively high-utility bid while making visible concessions on my lower-weight issues D and E. The opponent’s offers and messages are mostly pressure tactics, and their bids have not shown consistent improvement for me, but they have repeatedly signaled issueD:valueE and recently issueE:valueD, so I am conceding there while holding firm on my high-value issueA, issueB, and issueC positions.

At t=0.18 it is still early, so I am targeting a relatively high-utility bid while making visible concessions on lower-weight issues. The opponent’s offers remain low for me and their threats look like cheap talk, but they have repeatedly emphasized values around issueD/issueE and recently gave me issueC:valueB, so I will hold firm on issueA/issueB/issueC and concede more on the less important issues.

I am targeting a still-high utility around 0.75 because it is early at t=0.20 and the opponent’s offers remain very low-value to me with only small, inconsistent concessions. Their messages are mostly threats and urgency claims, so I am relying on the bid history instead; I will concede on low-weight issues D/E while holding firm on my high-weight issues A/B/C.

At t=0.22 it is still early, so I am targeting a high but slightly more conciliatory bid around strong utility for me rather than accepting the opponent’s very low-utility offers. The opponent’s offers and messages show hard bargaining with repeated threats, but their bids suggest they care about pushing several issues toward valueE/valueD, so I concede mainly on lower-weight issueD/issueE and make a moderate concession on issueC while holding firm on high-weight issueA and issueB.

I am targeting a strong but not maximal utility bid around 0.74, keeping my high-weight priorities on issueA and issueB while conceding substantially on the lower-weight issueD and issueE. At t=0.24 there is still time, so I should not accept the opponent’s low-utility offers, but their repeated bids suggest they may value issueD/issueE concessions and can tolerate issueC=valueB. Their messages are mostly pressure tactics, while the actual offers show only limited movement toward my preferences.

I targeted a utility around 0.65: a meaningful concession from my earlier high-utility bids while still protecting the most important issues. At t=0.26 there is some pressure to move, but it is still early enough not to accept the opponent’s very low-utility offers; their threats look like cheap talk, while their bids show only limited movement. I conceded on low-weight issueD/issueE and partially on high-weight issueA, while holding firm on issueB and issueC.

At t=0.28 I am still targeting a strong utility while making a clearer concession than my prior bid, mainly on lower-weight issues and on issueC where the opponent has repeatedly offered valueD/valueB. The opponent’s threats are cheap talk, but their bids show persistent pressure for issueB=valueE and issueD/issueE concessions; I will hold firm on high-value issueA and issueB for now while moving issueC, issueD, and issueE toward values they have signaled.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.70 at t=0.30, conceding mainly on lower-weight issues and making a moderate move on issueB while holding my highest-weight issueA and valuable issueC. The opponent’s messages are mostly pressure tactics, but their offers show they strongly prefer lower values on issueB/issueD/issueE and have occasionally offered issueA=valueA and issueC=valueB/D, so this bid moves toward their pattern without over-conceding early.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.69 by holding firm on my highest-weight issueA and preferred issueC, while conceding on lower-weight issueD and issueE and partially on issueB. At t=0.32 there is some time pressure but it is not late enough to accept the opponent’s lopsided bids; their offers show movement on issueB and issueC, while their threats appear repetitive cheap talk rather than binding constraints.

I am targeting a moderate concession around 0.61 utility for me: I keep issueA at my strongest value because it is my highest-weight issue, while conceding on lower-weight issueD/issueE and moving issueC toward a value the opponent has repeatedly offered. At t=0.34 there is still time, so I should not accept their low-utility threats at face value, but their bids show some real movement on issueA/issueB/issueC compared with their opening. Their messages are mostly pressure tactics, so I rely on the offer trend rather than the “walk away” claims.

I am targeting a moderate utility around 0.60: I concede on issueA to valueB and keep issueC at valueB because the opponent has recently signaled those may be acceptable, while asking for issueB valueB rather than their usual valueE. At t=0.36 there is still time, so I should not accept their pressure-filled messages at face value, but their offers do show gradual movement toward my preferences on issueA and issueC.

I am targeting a moderate utility around 0.63, since we are still before the midpoint but the opponent’s latest offer has become substantially better for me by conceding on issueA and issueC. Their messages are mostly threats of deadlock, so I am relying on the offer pattern instead: I keep their latest acceptable-looking positions on issueA, issueC, issueD, and issueE, while asking for a small improvement on issueB where they have previously offered valueD.

I am targeting a mid-game utility around 0.66, conceding mainly on lower/medium-priority issues while holding issueA and issueC because they are important to me and the opponent has already offered those favorable values at times. At t=0.40 there is pressure to move, but not enough to accept their latest low-utility offer; their repeated threats are cheap talk, while the offer history shows some willingness to give issueA=valueA and issueC=valueB. I changed my prior bid slightly and incorporated their current signal on issueE=valueC while keeping issueB at a concessionary valueD.

I am targeting a mid-game utility around 0.71, conceding mainly on lower-importance issues and on issueB while holding firm on my most valuable issueA and issueC. The opponent’s threats look like cheap talk, but their offers show some movement toward valueA on issueA and valueB on issueC, so this bid uses several values they have already signaled while improving over their latest offer for me.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.70 while making a meaningful mid-game concession at t=0.44. The opponent’s threats look like cheap-talk repetition, but their latest offers show movement on issueA and issueB while they continue to favor valueE on issueD/issueE, so I concede those lower-weight issues and keep my high-priority issueC at valueB.
